top_signals.title: Asia-US container rates face upward pressure; falling fuel costs could cap increases
top_signals.summary: Rates for shipping containers from Asia to the US continue to face upward pressure amid an early start to peak season and the pulling forward of volumes to be new tariffs, but lower bunker fuel costs could help slow the increases. top_signals.title: Goldman Axes $500 From Gold Target on No Fed Cuts This Year
top_signals.summary: Goldman Sachs Group Inc. cut its year-end gold forecast by $500 an ounce as the Federal Reserve is no longer seen easing in 2026.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: The Tonnage Math Behind a Hormuz Reopening
top_signals.summary: A Hormuz reopening is unlikely to trigger an immediate VLCC tonnage squeeze, as ample ballast capacity is already positioning towards the MEG, whereas diverting ballasters from the Atlantic could further add to the tally. top_signals.title: Safeguarding the future of green ports: NTUA secures grant to advance safe and resilient infrastructure for maritime electrification
top_signals.summary: A grant from Lloyd’s Register Foundation launches the AEGIS project to further enhance the resilience and operational safety of maritime electrification. Addresses one of the last technical hurdles to Onshore Power Supply technology and fosters confidence in green technologies.
